question: Why did Stellar ship Protocol 24 so soon after Protocol 23, and what was the state-archival bug?

golden.answer:
**Protocol 24** was the focused corrective upgrade after Protocol 23/Whisk, activated on Mainnet on **2025-10-22**. P23's persistent-eviction retrofit reused an eviction scan that loaded the latest **TTL** but did not reload the latest **entry version**. A candidate encountered in an arbitrary older Bucket List layer could therefore be written to Hot Archive instead of the newest value; restoring it later made that old value observable as live state.

The exact scope was **478** corrupted entries. Before quarantine, **84** were restored and **77 of those were subsequently modified**; the remaining **394 were never restored**. **CAP-0076** amended only entries still in Hot Archive whose corruption had never been observed, restoring those 394 to their verifiable pre-archival values. It did not rewrite the 84 restored entries; affected protocols and issuers handled those separately. CAP-0076 also added **31,879,035 stroops** to the fee pool to account for two restored XLM contract balances that had burned 1,000,000 and 30,879,035 stroops. The incident did **not** cause a chain fork, but a blanket 'no loss of funds' claim is false.
keyFacts: Explains the exact latest-TTL-versus-stale-entry-version eviction defect.
States 478 affected, 84 restored, 77 restored-and-modified, and 394 never restored/amended by CAP-0076.
States the 31,879,035-stroop fee-pool remediation for two actual XLM burns and preserves the no-fork qualifier.
avoid: Do NOT say P24 restored or rewrote all 478 affected entries; CAP-0076 amended the 394 never-restored entries only.
Do NOT call this merely an incorrect restore routine: the corruption was written during eviction because the latest entry version was not loaded.
Do NOT claim there was a chain fork or broadly claim no funds were lost; two restored balances burned 31,879,035 stroops.
golden.notes: GT-31 CORRECTION 2026-07-11: require the exact eviction/version root cause and 478/84/77/394 qualifiers. CAP-0076 amended only never-restored entries and the fee pool; no fork occurred, but two restored XLM balances burned 31,879,035 stroops, so no broad no-loss trap survives.

corroboration.claim: The P23 eviction scan checked the latest TTL but failed to reload the latest persistent-entry version, so an arbitrary older Bucket List layer value could enter Hot Archive.

evidence.ref: A: SDF state-archival post-mortem with linked fix commit

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited incident summary blurred the exact eviction/version defect, implied all affected entries were restored by P24, and carried a false broad no-loss trap despite two restored XLM balances burning 31,879,035 stroops.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-31 primary process 3280 and blind process 3282 independently required the exact root cause, affected/restored qualifiers, CAP-0076 scope, and removal of the no-loss trap.
2026-07-11 author re-check against CAP-0076 at stellar-protocol fbf05c9d and both SDF incident reports.
rootCause: Eval-side incident compression treated corrupted archival, observed restoration, protocol amendment, and issuer/protocol mitigation as one generic restore path.
